name: 14-malformed-sse
description: Canonical scenario #14: malformed SSE chunk. The emulator opens a 200 SSE stream and emits message_start (or, on OR, response.created + response.in_progress + output_item.added) correctly, then writes a delta-typed event whose data payload is intentionally invalid JSON (`{"text": "\xZZ-not-valid-json}`). Both SDKs' incremental JSON parsers must surface a typed parse error and stop — not crash. The comparator uses the same maximally-relaxed config as #13 (ignoreThrown + skipTerminalCheck + skipEventStreamCheck + skipHookOrderCheck): the two SDKs differ on whether they project a parse error into a thrown error vs. a typed terminal event, and ambiguity call #1 in the issue body authorizes asserting by error TYPE (typed parse error reaches host) rather than by exact message text. Per the brief's '8+ ambiguity calls' requirement, this scenario also encodes the call that 'malformed JSON in the SSE payload, not in the surrounding SSE envelope' is what malformed_delta / malformed_event actually inject — a stricter envelope-corruption mode would require new emulator wiring and is out of scope.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.
